response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced senior engineer with genuine strengths in web platforms, testing culture, and knowledge sharing, but he does not meet the core minimum qualifications for this role. The position requires 8 years of C++; his resume shows roughly a year of professional C/C++ (National Instruments, plus a thesis project), with his systems-level work concentrated in DLang instead. High-scale experience exists (Sociomantic's microservices handling millions of requests per second) but spans only about two years, and there is no evidence of formal technical leadership of cloud or platform teams — his leadership is mentoring via workshops and courses rather than owning technical strategy for an internal compute platform.
strengths: Real high-throughput, low-latency systems experience at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic (millions of HTTP requests per second)
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and authored testing frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial)
Proven mentor and educator — created multi-day courses, workshops, and conference talks
concerns: Far short of the required 8 years of C++ — professional C++ use is limited to ~7 months plus academic work
No formal technical-leadership role leading cloud/platform teams or setting cross-team technical strategy
Limited production cloud-architecture depth — cloud exposure is mostly application-level (AWS CDK, Kubernetes deployment) rather than building compute infrastructure

key_factors.factor: Lack of formal technical leadership experience
